data.title: Stagwell names CEO to spearhead Harris Quest
data.description:
• Stagwell (STGW) appointed Jonathan Gardner as CEO of The Marketing Cloud's and The Harris Poll's Harris Quest.
  • Harris Quest is The Marketing Cloud (formerly Stagwell Marketing Cloud) and The Harris Poll's unified brand, offering AI-enabled research and marketing technology solutions. 
  • Reporting to the Marketing Cloud's CEO Elspeth Rollert, Gardner will spearhead Harris Quest's market research operations. He brings over 30 years of experience in market research and polling.
  • "Gardner brings deep expertise to his new role, having spent nearly a decade at The Harris Poll as COO, overseeing all aspects of client fulfillment at the industry-leading research and consulting firm." the company said.

data.title: First Majestic Silver launches $300M convertible notes offering
data.description:
• First Majestic Silver (AG) is launching a private offering of $300 million in unsecured convertible notes due 2031, with an option for buyers to purchase an additional $45 million.
  • The notes will bear cash interest semi-annually at a fixed rate and will be convertible by holders into First Majestic common shares.
  • The company plans to use the proceeds to buy back part of its 0.375% convertible notes due 2027 and for general corporate purposes.
  • AG -1% premarket.

data.title: Grocery Outlet warns of flat Q4 comps, sees EPS at low end of forecast
data.description:
Grocery Outlet (GO) on Wednesday said, based on recent sales trends, the company now expects comparable store sales to be approximately flat and expects its diluted adjusted earnings per share to be at the low end of its previous forecast range of $0.21 to $0.23 in the fourth quarter.

For fiscal 2025, the company now expects comparable store sales growth at the low end of the previously issued guidance range of 0.6% to 0.9% and diluted adjusted earnings per share to be at the low end of the previously issued guidance range of $0.78 to $0.80, as per a filing.

Analysts on average expect Q4 EPS of $0.22/share on revenue of $1.23B. For the full year, consensus estimates call for earnings of $0.79 per share, up 2.49%, and revenue of $4.70 billion, a 7.51% increase.

data.title: Telus sees $2.15B FCF in 2025; dividend hikes on hold until stock recovers
data.description:
• Telus (TU)  said Wednesday it expects to generate roughly $2.15 billion in free cash flow in 2025 and is aiming for at least 10% annual FCF growth from 2026 to 2028.
  • The company offered preliminary guidance for 2026, projecting about $2.4 billion in free cash flow and roughly $2.3 billion in capital expenditures.
  • The company will systematically step down its Discounted DRIP starting in early 2026 and pause dividend growth while continuing to pay its $0.4184 per-share quarterly dividend at the current level, it said in a statement.
  • "Collectively, these undertakings reinforce our strong progress on deleveraging, with our leverage ratio expected to reach approximately 3.3-times by the end of 2026 as we advance toward our target of approximately 3-times by the end of 2027," Darren Entwistle, President and CEO of Telus (TU) said.

data.title: Sprinklr Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.03, revenue of $219.07M beats by $9.51M
data.description:
• Sprinklr press release (CXM): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $219.07M (+9.2% Y/Y) beats by $9.51M.
  • Q3 Subscription Revenue of $190.3 million, up 5% year-over-year
  • RPO down 5% and cRPO up 3% year-over-year
  • 145 $1 million customers

  • Sprinklr is providing the following guidance for the fourth fiscal quarter ending January 31, 2026:

    • Subscription revenue between $191 million and $192 million.
    • Total revenue between $216.5 million and $217.5 million.
    • Non-GAAP operating income between $29 million and $30 million.
    • Non-GAAP net income per share of between $0.09 and $0.10, assuming 254 million diluted weighted-average shares outstanding.

  • Sprinklr is providing the following guidance for the full fiscal year ending January 31, 2026:

    • Subscription revenue between $754 million and $755 million.
    • Total revenue between $853 million and $854 million.
    • Non-GAAP operating income between $137.5 million and $138.5 million.
    • Non-GAAP net income per share between $0.43 and $0.44, assuming 265 million diluted weighted-average shares outstanding.

data.title: Mortgage demand down despite lower rates
data.description:
• MBA Mortgage Applications
  • Composite Index: -1.4% vs. +0.2% a week ago
  • 30-year fixed-rate mortgages came in at 6.32%, compared to 6.40% last week

data.title: Enbridge raises dividend by 3% to CAD 0.97
data.description:
• Enbridge (ENB) declares CAD 0.97/share quarterly dividend, 2.9% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.94.
  • Forward yield 5.77%
  • Payable March 1; for shareholders of record Feb. 17; ex-div Feb. 17.
  • See ENB Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Dollar Tree gains after pointing to momentum across its business
data.description:
Dollar Tree (DLTR) reported sales were up 9.4% to $4.6 billion in Q3. Same-store net sales growth was +4.2% for the Dollar Tree banner to match the consensus estimate. The same-store sales gain was driven by a 4.5% increase in traffic and a 0.3% decline in average ticket. The retailer pointed to strong momentum across the business.

Gross profit increased 10.8% to $1.7 billion during the quarter, and the gross margin rate expanded 40 basis points to 35.8%. The expansion in gross margin was driven primarily by improved mark-on from pricing initiatives, lower domestic and import freight costs, and a favorable sales mix, partially offset by higher tariff costs, markdowns, and shrink. Adjusted operating income increased 4.1% to $345.3 million. EPS was reported at $1.21 vs. $1.08 consensus.

At the end of the quarter, Dollar Tree (DLTR) had approximately $2.0 billion remaining under its $2.5 billion share repurchase authorization, $594.8 million of cash and cash equivalents, $300 million of commercial paper notes outstanding, and no borrowings under its revolvers.

Dollar Tree (DLTR) opened 106 new stores during the quarter and converted approximately 646 stores to the 3.0 multi-price format.

Looking ahead, Dollar Tree (DLTR) guided for full-year EPS of $5.60 to $5.80 (midpoint $5.70) vs. $5.51 consensus and a prior outlook for $5.32 to $5.72. The outlook assumes that the level of tariffs in place today remains in effect for the balance of the fiscal year. "Our multi-price strategy drove strong momentum across our business in the third quarter and helped deliver an all-time record Halloween season. Today’s Dollar Tree is a preferred destination for a wide range of shoppers – whether they rely on us for everyday essentials, appreciate a fast and easy trip, or enjoy the excitement of discovering something unexpected," noted CEO Mike Creedon.

Shares of Dollar Tree (DLTR) rose 2.8% in premarket trading to $112.00. The 52-week high for the discounter stock is $118.06. Short interest on DLTR stands at 9.2% of the total float. Rival Dollar General (DG) was up 0.4% in the early session.

data.title: The Crypto Company plans to eliminate $4M convertible debt
data.description:
• The Crypto Company (OTCPK:CRCW) entered into a restructuring agreement with its longtime financing partner, AJB Capital Investments, with the intent of removing ~$4 million of legacy convertible debt from its balance sheet.
  • "AJB supported this company at a time when access to capital across the digital-asset industry was extremely limited. This restructuring, once closed, will mark an important chapter in our capital structure and will give us a far cleaner runway to execute our long-term strategy. We are deeply appreciative of AJB's role in getting the company to this point." said Ron Levy, CEO of The Crypto Company.
